Name: Talisman's Special Solutions: Task Management Ontology
Description: A real-world workflow demonstrating task management, agent assignments, and basic aggregations.

ProjectSettings.Name: project-name
ProjectSettings.Value: Talisman's Special Solutions: Task Management Ontology

ProjectTranspilers.Name: ensureorchestrator
ProjectTranspilers.Description: Ensure the local ssotme-proxy orchestrator (localhost:4242 transpiler bus) is running so the repo-local http://localhost:4242/* tools below (rulebook-to-owl, postgres-calculated-to-rulebook) are reachable. Idempotent: boots the proxy only if it is not already live.

ProjectTranspilers.Name: rulebooktopostgres
ProjectTranspilers._ClosureNote: The Postgres transpiler emits the transitive-closure views. postgres-bootstrap/03-create-views.sql (committed) contains both vw_step_precedence_closure and vw_roles_closure as WITH RECURSIVE views with is_inferred/hop_distance columns, and the committed SQL is what the demo runs against — closure is present out of the box. (_LocalDevUrl is an optional pointer for running the transpiler locally during tool development; it is not needed to build or run this demo.)
ProjectTranspilers.Description: Generate Postgres schema + seed data from the rulebook

ProjectTranspilers.Name: execute
ProjectTranspilers._Note: Reseed the reasoner's data store (app/backend/db.json) from the rulebook on EVERY build, parallel to init-db.sh for Postgres. Runs AFTER init-db.sh so the live DB columns exist for the authoritative raw/derived classification. Without this, db.json drifts: a raw field with a default added to the rulebook (e.g. RenewalWindowMonths=2) never lands in the stale db.json, the reasoner reads null, and the UI shows COMPLIANT when AT RISK. start.sh does NOT reseed, so only a build fixes it.

ProjectTranspilers.Name: httplocalhost4242postgrescalculatedtorulebook
ProjectTranspilers._IsDisabledNote: INTENTIONALLY DISABLED guardrail. This reverse spoke refreshes derived-field DISPLAY copies in the rulebook from Postgres views. It is kept off because an earlier run wrote stale step DATA back into the sacred rulebook from a prod-built DB and overwrote a curated re-curation of the workflow steps. Keep IsDisabled:true until the spoke is verified to write derived/display fields ONLY (never raw step data) AND the DB has been rebuilt cleanly from a closure-enabled tool. The shipped model is the 5-step Production Deployment Workflow (see README).
ProjectTranspilers.Description: Reverse spoke: refresh rulebook derived-field display copies from Postgres views after the destructive rebuild (raws verified, never written).

ProjectTranspilers.Name: rulebooktoreactexplainerdag
ProjectTranspilers._HeadPointerNote: Pinned to the explicit v2026.06.11.1530 cpln URL (NOT the bare tool name) because [latest] still resolves to the older v2026.06.10.1301 — the publish on 2026-06-11 built+activated v1530 but the HeadVersion pointer did not flip to it. v1530 is the version carrying the hovercard portal/edge-clamp fix (DagHoverCard renders via createPortal to document.body, fixed-positioned + four-edge viewport-clamped). Re-point CommandLine back to the bare 'rulebook-to-react-explainer-dag' once [latest] correctly advances to v1530 or newer.
ProjectTranspilers.Description: Generate the self-contained React Explainer DAG module (app/frontend/src/explainer-dag/) — embedded rulebook + dagResolver + DagCell/DagToggle/FieldChip/FieldDag — the canonical, fully-generated provenance visualizer. Replaces the former hand-written explain-dag/ module.
